buggy_code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

features = pd.DataFrame({
    'momentum': [0.01, -0.02, 0.03, 0.01, -0.01],
    'volume': [1000, 1200, 800, 1100, 900]
}, index=pd.date_range('2020-01-01', periods=5))

target = pd.Series(
    [0.02, -0.01, 0.04, 0.00, -0.02],
    index=pd.date_range('2020-01-01', periods=5)
)

# Sort features
features_sorted = features.sort_values('momentum', ascending=False)

# Realign target to match sorted index
target_aligned = target.loc[features_sorted.index]

X = features_sorted.values
y = target_aligned.values

assert len(X) == len(y)
print('Feature-target pairs aligned correctly')
clean_code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

features = pd.DataFrame({
    'momentum': [0.01, -0.02, 0.03, 0.01, -0.01],
    'volume': [1000, 1200, 800, 1100, 900]
}, index=pd.date_range('2020-01-01', periods=5))

target = pd.Series(
    [0.02, -0.01, 0.04, 0.00, -0.02],
    index=pd.date_range('2020-01-01', periods=5)
)

features_sorted = features.sort_values('momentum', ascending=False)
target_aligned = target.loc[features_sorted.index]

X = features_sorted.values
y = target_aligned.values

assert len(X) == len(y)
print('Feature-target pairs aligned correctly')
description: Clean example — target realigned to sorted feature index before extracting values.
explanation: After sort_values(), target is realigned using .loc[features_sorted.index] to match the new row order. Both arrays extracted to numpy only after alignment is confirmed.
